title: Fourier Analysis, Signals, and Time Series · Worked Case: Filtering Convolution
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving signals and temporal demand. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math adv filtering convolution increment in a reproducible signals fourier computational study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate filtering convolution in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute sampling aliasing in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Filtering Convolution evidence
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 9 units and 12 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(9² + 12²) and check that the result exceeds either component.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
